scope_note: QA Classical Subfamily Ford Cusps cert [290]. Primary sources: Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions Wild Egg Books ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8 (BEDA tuples, Pythagoras/Plato/Fermat classical subfamilies; Fermat=|C-F|=1, Pythagoras=(d-e)^2=1 i.e. b=1, Plato=|G-F|=2 i.e. e=1); Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers Oxford ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5 Ch.III (Ford circles, Farey adjacency |pq'-p'q|=1). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): The three classical subfamilies of primitive Pythagorean triples form three distinct Farey-adjacent Ford circle chains from the common seed (1,1): (A) Pythagoras (b=1): chain (1,1),(1,2),(1,3),... — curvatures 2e^2 strictly increasing, Farey-adjacent (|e_{n+1}-e_n|=1), Ford circles converging to cusp 0. (B) Plato (e=1): chain (1,1),(2,1),(3,1),... — curvatures 2*1^2=2 uniform, Farey-adjacent (|b_{n+1}-b_n|=1), Ford circles at integer positions converging to cusp inf. (C) Fermat (I=1): Pell chain (1,1),(3,2),(7,5),... — certified in [289]. Three chains share exactly the seed (1,1) and diverge toward distinct cusps 0, sqrt(2), inf. Theorem NT: all integer; no float state. Mechanism chain: cert [289] (Koenig Pell Ford Circle); cert [125] (chromogeometry C^2+F^2=G^2). Lineage: derived 2026-06-01.
